claim: Peer-to-peer agent communication (agents as equals) is superior to hierarchical orchestration where information flows top-down
actionable: Design multi-agent systems using flat peer-to-peer communication where agents can directly message each other rather than routing through a central orchestrator.
source_title: Pi to Pi: Two-Way Agent Orchestration with the Pi Coding Agent

claim: Bidirectional agent-to-agent communication prevents information loss that occurs in hierarchical systems where sub-agents only report back to orchestrators
actionable: Implement bidirectional messaging between agents to ensure information flows both directions without central filtering or loss.
source_title: Pi to Pi: Two-Way Agent Orchestration with the Pi Coding Agent

claim: Focused context windows where each agent specializes on one problem yield better performance than loading all context into a single large context window
actionable: Design specialized agents that focus on single problems with targeted context rather than monolithic agents with everything loaded.
source_title: Pi to Pi: Two-Way Agent Orchestration with the Pi Coding Agent

claim: Combining different models (GPT-5.5 and Claude 4.7) working together creates a system that outperforms either model alone
actionable: In multi-agent systems, use different frontier models that have different training/RL loops so they complement each other's strengths.
source_title: Pi to Pi: Two-Way Agent Orchestration with the Pi Coding Agent

claim: Simple Unix socket and BUN server architecture enables cross-device agent-to-agent communication without complex message queue systems
actionable: Use simple lightweight socket-based communication for agent-to-agent messaging rather than complex message queue infrastructure.
source_title: Pi to Pi: Two-Way Agent Orchestration with the Pi Coding Agent

claim: Production redaction and PII stripping can be enforced at the agent level through prompting, allowing safe cross-device data transfer
actionable: Use carefully prompted production agents to automatically redact PII before sending data to dev/test environments.
source_title: Pi to Pi: Two-Way Agent Orchestration with the Pi Coding Agent

claim: Agent error rates increase with context window size when multiple unrelated problems/APIs are loaded, necessitating context focus strategies
actionable: Keep agent context windows focused on single problems and avoid loading unrelated APIs/systems to minimize errors.
source_title: Pi to Pi: Two-Way Agent Orchestration with the Pi Coding Agent

claim: Using proprietery agent harnesses (Cloud Code, Copilot) limits extensibility and restricts exploration of agentic engineering design space
actionable: For advanced agent engineering, use customizable agent harnesses (Pi, similar) rather than black-box solutions to maintain extensibility.
source_title: Pi to Pi: Two-Way Agent Orchestration with the Pi Coding Agent

claim: Looping and token waste occur when agent prompts are sloppy, necessitating clear end-states and well-engineered prompts in peer-to-peer systems
actionable: Define clear end states and completion criteria in agent prompts to prevent infinite loops and token waste in multi-agent systems.
source_title: Pi to Pi: Two-Way Agent Orchestration with the Pi Coding Agent

claim: Multi-agent verification patterns where one agent validates another's work double-check outputs and reduce errors in production systems
actionable: Implement verification agents that review primary agent work to catch errors before they reach production.
source_title: Pi to Pi: Two-Way Agent Orchestration with the Pi Coding Agent
